Alpha Probe Mission Summary
The Alpha probe surveyed a small rocky body in the outer belt over a six-month campaign, mapping its surface and sampling its thin exosphere.
Its instruments measured a surface gravity of 3.71 m/s^2, consistent with the body's estimated density. The value anchors the mission's mass model.
The probe remains in a parking orbit awaiting an extended-mission decision.
--- end fetched content ---
